samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_16bit">
    <description>Design a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit numbers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst">Reset signal.</input>
        <input id="sign">Signed or unsigned operation.</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[15:0]">Input operand DIVIDEND.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[15:0]">Input operand DIVISOR.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">Valid operation request.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">The output has been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">The result is valid.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">Output result.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="IDLE">0</param>
        <param id="START">1</param>
        <param id="SUB">2</param>
        <param id="UPDATE_SR">3</param>
        <param id="DONE">4</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="div_valid">
            if (clk)
            div_valid <= opn_valid && !res_valid;
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="sign_reg">
            if (clk)
            sign_reg <= sign;
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="dividend_reg">
            if (clk)
            dividend_reg <= dividend;
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="divisor_reg">
            if (clk)
            divisor_reg <= divisor;
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="cnt">
            if (clk)
            begin
                if (!res_valid)
                    cnt <= 1;
                else if (cnt[3])
                    cnt <= 0;
                else
                    cnt <= cnt + 1;
            </end>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="start_cnt">
            if (clk)
            begin
                if (!res_valid)
                    start_cnt <= 1;
                else
                    start_cnt <= 0;
            end
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="res_valid">
            if (clk)
            begin
                if (rst)
                    res_valid <= 0;
                else if (cnt[3])
                    res_valid <= 1;
                else if (res_valid && res_ready)
                    res_valid <= 0;
                else
                    res_valid <= res_valid;
            end
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="res_valid">
            if (clk)
            begin
                if (rst)
                    res_valid <= 0;
                else if (cnt[3])
                    res_valid <= 1;
                else if (res_valid && res_ready)
                    res_valid <= 0;
                else
                    res_valid <= res_valid;
            end
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="result">
            if (clk)
            begin
                if (cnt[3])
                    result <= SR[15:0];
                else
                    result <= result;
            end
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module performs division operation when opn_valid is high and res_valid is low. It saves the inputs dividend and divisor, initializes the shift register SR with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit, sets NEG_DIVISOR to the negated absolute value of the divisor, and sets the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process. If start_cnt is high, the module performs the following steps: If the counter cnt reaches 8, the division is complete. cnt and start_cnt are cleared. Updates the shift register SR with the final remainder and quotient. Otherwise, the counter cnt is incremented, and the shift register SR is updated based on the subtraction result: Computes the subtraction of NEG_DIVISOR. Uses a multiplexer to select the appropriate result based on the carry-out. Updates SR by shifting left and inserting the carry-out.
    </implementation>
</module>
